slides.title: Sports, Olympics, and Achievement
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 7 English, Unit 6: Sports, Olympics, and Achievement. Welcome to Unit 6! This unit is all about sports, the Olympics, and what it means to achieve something great. You will explore how athletes train, how the Olympic Games began in ancient Greece, and how the Paralympics have changed our understanding of what the human body can do. Along the way you will build powerful vocabulary, strengthen your grammar, and learn to write about sports with confidence and skill. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and understand non-fiction texts about sports and the Olympic Games
Use important vocabulary such as analogy, bias, evidence, viewpoint, and more
Recognise and form compound nouns (e.g. football, swimming pool, team-mate)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and understand non-fiction texts about sports and the olympic games. you will also use important vocabulary such as analogy, bias, evidence, viewpoint, and more. you will also recognise and form compound nouns (e.g. football, swimming pool, team-mate). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: analogy, argumentative, bias, evidence, objective, viewpoint. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Sports, Olympics, and Achievement: source text 1
Sports, Olympics, and Achievement: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Sports, Olympics, and Achievement: source text 1 and Sports, Olympics, and Achievement: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Compound Nouns
bullets: What is a compound noun? A compound noun is made by combining two or more words to create a new noun with a specific meaning. Understanding compound nouns...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Compound Nouns. What is a compound noun? A compound noun is made by combining two or more words to create a new noun with a specific meaning. Understanding compound nouns helps you express yourself precisely and makes your writing richer. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Sports Debate. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
